reasoning: Based on authoritative sources, gold reached $4,000/oz on October 8, 2025 and remained at ~$3,900-4,000 in early November after an 8.5-11% correction from mid-October peaks above $4,380. The $3,200 threshold is $700-800 (20-25%) below current levels. Strong fundamentals support continued elevated prices: dollar weakness, Fed rate cut expectations, geopolitical tensions, and record central bank buying (219.9 tonnes in Q3, +28% vs Q2). While a 20% correction to exactly $3,200 is theoretically possible (discussed in analyst reports), the probability is low given bullish institutional forecasts (UBS: $4,200, Goldman/ING: $4,200-5,600) and strong structural demand. With only 7 weeks until year-end, gold would need to fall >18% from current levels, which would require a major negative catalyst not evident in the data.
